title: RTSP to YouTube Live streaming via FFmpeg in Docker
context: This is the core and only purpose of the bobbin project: take an RTSP stream and relay it to YouTube Live using FFmpeg. The solution must be containerized using Alpine Linux for minimal image size, configured via environment variables, and deployable via Docker Compose with automatic restart on failure.

tasks.title: Create Dockerfile with Alpine Linux and FFmpeg
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile exists at project root
Base image is alpine:latest
FFmpeg is installed via apk add --no-cache ffmpeg
ENV declarations exist for RTSP_URL and STREAM_KEY with empty defaults
CMD uses exec form so FFmpeg runs as PID 1 and receives SIGTERM correctly
CMD runs ffmpeg with: -nostdin, -rtsp_transport tcp, -i using $RTSP_URL, -c:v copy, -c:a aac -b:a 128k, -f flv output to rtmp://a.rtmp.youtube.com/live2/$STREAM_KEY
docker build . completes successfully and produces an image under 100MB

tasks.title: Create Docker Compose configuration
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose.yml exists at project root
Defines a single service named youtube-stream
Service builds from the local Dockerfile (build: .)
container_name is set to rtsp-youtube-stream
restart policy is unless-stopped
Environment variables RTSP_URL and STREAM_KEY are defined with placeholder values
docker compose config validates successfully (no syntax errors)

tasks.title: Add .env.example and .gitignore for secrets management
acceptance_criteria: .env.example exists with RTSP_URL and STREAM_KEY placeholder entries and comments explaining each
.gitignore exists and includes .env to prevent accidental secret commits
docker-compose.yml references env_file or environment variables that can be overridden by a .env file

tasks.title: Add README with usage instructions
acceptance_criteria: README.md exists at project root
Documents the project purpose (RTSP to YouTube Live relay)
Includes prerequisites (Docker, Docker Compose)
Includes setup steps: copy .env.example to .env, fill in values
Includes commands to build, start (docker compose up -d), check logs (docker logs -f rtsp-youtube-stream), and stop
Documents the two required environment variables (RTSP_URL, STREAM_KEY) with descriptions
